facts.text: Babylon was named in 1803 by Nathaniel Conklin after the ancient city of Babylon, echoing the historic name that inspired the town's identity.
The Long Island Rail Road's Babylon Branch, opening in 1867, made the village a seaside resort hub and brought vacationers to the Great South Bay.
Captain Jacob Conklin built the first house in 1710 at what is now Wheatley Heights, marking the earliest settlement in Babylon.
The Conklin house, moved in 1871, now serves as a museum on Deer Park Avenue, preserving early Babylon history.
In the 1960s, actor Rodney Dangerfield and comedian Dan Meuser lived in North Babylon, adding celebrity flavor to the community.
The town's official seal was authorized on April 2, 1878, symbolizing Babylon's formal establishment after its 1872 creation.
